reviews.review_msg: Super anal about plagiarism, had her for tutorial leader as well and she would make you sign a consent form about the issue of "I will not cheat". She has high expectations, 1st year students will probably have a hard time in her class coming out of gr.12

Hardest marker ever. This class will ruin your self-esteem when it comes to writing essays and exams. She tries to be funny, but she's completely horrible. Her jokes suck! Avoid this prof if you don't want to receive bad marks - seriously.
She's boring

I thought she was a fantastic prof! Tried her best making leactures interesting. However, I think your TA is your best bet to get a good mark! My TA was awesome so I got quite a good mark. Avoid her tutorial. I would take her class again! Exams are very straight forward :)
Just AVOID her.....

Way too strict and expectations are way too high for a 100 level English course. Not to be rude or anything, but the whole "what is the significance, what are the implications" is going way over. The books are boring in the first place, and setting the bar that high is just tormenting the students. The TAs were not helpful in terms of assignments.
Her expectation are ridiculous! Unless you are a very talented writer, you would hate this class. If you are a decent writer, then this class will smash your self esteem and your GPA.
I didnt like her at all
Do not ever take her class! She is unfair and her lectures are useless and irrelevant!

God she's awful. Not to be rude, but she makes the class way more difficult than it needs to be. I have taken other 1st year English classes before, but she has set the bars way too high in her class when it comes to the Essays. If you essay has the quality of a B paper and 1 quality of a C paper u end up with a C for the paper and thats that!
Great class if you love reading like I do. TA's are super helpful. Del Vento is quirky, kinda sorta crazy, but makes class fun. Assignments and exams are fair. Good 100 level english to take.
All she does it read off her power points. Her class was not useful to me at all. She was a nice teacher, but I did not learn a thing in her class.

All she really does is read off her ppts, I think she is a nice teacher, but sometimes I do not find attending her class useful. During her exams it's YOUR analysis, so what she writes on her ppts is very very broad, and not always the most helpful. Books were not that great either unfortunately.

she was good prof and i dont know what the other people are talking about she paying student to give her good ratings. In my opinion if you focus during lecture you do not even need to read the books. In exams as long you mention the points she mentions in class you score good. Overall good class. P.S. try not to get into her tut section.

Professor P.D. is by far one of the nicest teachers I have ever had in SFU. She can relate to you on any level and she's always willing to help. Attending her lectures can be useful if you want to understand more about the books but in the end, you don't get marks when you mention something on your test that was already said in class/tutorial.

quite posibly the best teacher I will ever have, she totaly goes above and beyond to help students write better and think about lit in a new way....her office hours are always full with other students but then again, can you say that about any of your other profs?

I'm sucked at writing, but she is a nice prof. She makes the grading policy very tough and if you get a tougher TA, things would never go into the right directions. I didn't see any improvements in my writing skills, but her class made me love reading fictions. Overall she is neither a great nor a bad prof.

I'd like to take the chance to say she is quite possibly one of my favourite professors here at SFU. She's very clear on the materials that are needed and her explanations are easy to follow. If you have the chance, never hesitate to take her class!!
amazing prof. if you give a damn about university, and actually try to learn she is awesome prof who caters to those students! I wish she could teach upper division English courses!

Worst prof ever for an introductory writing course! I loved English in high-school and I knew it wouldn't be as easy but honestly, she marks way too hard!! Lectures are irrelevant and she's not clear about what she wants on essays. Even went to her office hours, didn't help much. WORST EVER!! :@

She is the worst teacher ever. She does not know how to teach. Her lectures are very boring and she marks very hard. Do no take her class if you want a good mark in English. Everyone I know hates her too.

DONOT TAKE HER CLASS! I think she payed the students below to write those comments. She is horrible. If you want to do horribly bad in english then feel free to take her class. I have never hated english more than this.

In an ideal world, every teacher in every school would be a clone of Professor P.D. I want to clone her and send version "K-PD01" back to the past, so that she could have taught me during my high school years when I spent each lunch hour crying in the library. Had she been my teacher back then, I would have looked forward to school each morning...

Probably one of the best professor's at SFU. I looked forward to her class. She is extremely nice, loves to help, interactive, and a little quirky. There are a lot of readings but i really enjoyed the class. Dont pass the chance of being in her class.

Awesome Prof, I hate english, writing, and papers, and yet I was still able to pull an A- off through hard work with alot of her help.  Taking her course in my first year and first semester was probably one of the best choices I made at SFU, as she teaches you fundamentals of both reading and writing that peers in upper levels struggle with.
She's passionate with the materials and the subject she teaches. Quite easy to handle.

Don't pass the chance of being in her class because Professor P.D. is an excellent teacher! Very helpful and always willing to assist her students in their work. Professional, and comes to class very well prepared. Students can learn alot from attending her classes, that's for sure.
She is awesome! It's not easy stuff, but you can learn a lot from her! I have never had a professor who is as responsible as she is. Always there to help you! Best class I've ever had!

This professor is awesome! Don't give up the opportunity to be in her class. A lot of readings yes, but very friendly with marking. If you're struggling, you should go see her as she is very helpful. I got an A- with her and although you have to work for it, it is not impossible. A lot better then other english professors. Quizzes are straight forw

Lots of writing compared to the other eng199 classes, but it's all pretty useful stuff. She's hardcore english though, can talk about it for hours. And the textbook is DRY. Evil thing to read, but Prof. PD is very nice.
Pretty cool prof
No Comments
